post.content: Given a graph where each edge is one of the three types: A, B and C. A type edge can only be traversed by a man. B type edge can only be traversed by a woman and C type can be traversed by both.\n\nYou need to tell the maximum number of edges that can be removed from the graph so that it can still be fully traversed by both a man and a woman.

Solution - Let\'s assume that by not removing any edge, the graph is traversable by both - man and woman. Otherwise, there is no solution.\nLet the number of man edges be M and the number of woman edges be W. First, we will start adding all edges that can be traversed by both - man and woman, 1 by 1. Use the disjoint set union data structure for this. If by adding a new edge we connect 2 nodes in the same connected component, we discard that edge, otherwise, we add it. Let the number of edges discarded in this process be D. Now, you will have some number of connected components. Let this number be K. Now, only considering man edges, each non-redundant man edge should reduce the number of connected components by 1. Hence, minimum number of man edges that must be added to make sure that we are left with exactly 1 connected components at the end is K - 1. Hence we will be discarding M - (K - 1) man edges. We do the same thing for woman edges. Hence, we will be diacarding W - ( K - 1) woman edges. Hence total edges discarded = D + (W - (K - 1) )+ (M - (K - 1)).

Assuming the graph is undirected, we can use a disjoint set to detect redundant edges. What we want is a tree which is a connected graph with the minimum number of edges.
Pass1: for men, consider all A and C edges (Sort first by C and then A). Whenever an edge forms a cycle eliminate that edge. Let us denote the set of required edges as SM.
Pass1: for women, consider all B and C edges (Sort first by C and then B). Whenever an edge forms a cycle eliminate that edge. Let us denote the set of required edges as SW.
The answer will be [E - {SM union SW}].  Since we have started with C edges in both the cases, there will be overlap and those edges will be used once and SM union SW will be minimized hence E - {SM union SW} will be maximized.
Can you please update with an example ? Thanks!
